title: Purged race-level walk-forward CV with embargo
summary: `PitNextLap` uses a forward 1-lap horizon, so adjacent laps from the same driver/race create overlapping label windows and can make ordinary grouped CV mildly optimistic.
rationale: `PitNextLap` uses a forward 1-lap horizon, so adjacent laps from the same driver/race create overlapping label windows and can make ordinary grouped CV mildly optimistic. Financial ML literature treats this as a purging/embargo problem, and the same logic applies here because nearby laps are highly autocorrelated and label-overlapping.
implementation_hint: Split by chronological race blocks, keep whole races intact, and add a small embargo around fold boundaries or remove boundary rows per driver/race where train rows are within 1-2 laps of test rows. Use this CV for model selection and for any OOF stacking features.
expected_effect: May not raise raw CV immediately, but should reduce leaderboard surprise and make small gains from other ideas more trustworthy.
risk: If the competition test set is IID across races rather than chronological, stricter CV can lower local scores and understate achievable public LB performance.
